implemented_outputs.current_capability: imports verified APU RAM seeds, captures public register preflight state, probes the $0500 driver entry with ares' SPC700 disassembler, runs a timer0-aware diagnostic SPC700 shim plus APUIO0 track-command preseed past the old $FD wait into track-specific update code, and emits diagnostic SPC snapshots
builds a structurally valid SNES-SPC700 container from the diagnostic probe's APU RAM, SPC700 registers, and DSP registers; not yet treated as faithful playback state
indexes generated diagnostic SPC containers, header registers, RAM/DSP hashes, and declared output integrity for the representative corpus
exercises the snes_spc backend job/result adapter by copying validated diagnostic SPC inputs and writing readiness metadata without linking the renderer library yet
renders job-specified WAVs from fused CHANGE_MUSIC/key-on SPC snapshots through out-of-tree libgme; proves the lightweight renderer/export path while emulator-oracle comparison remains pending
summarizes rendered WAV energy and classifies diagnostic snapshot captures as silent, click/trace, very quiet, or audible
summarizes ares diagnostic probe behavior, including DSP key-on/key-off counts and the capture-boundary diagnosis
patches diagnostic SPC DSP KON/KOF registers from observed key-on data to test whether capture timing, not renderer plumbing, explains quiet renders
compares baseline diagnostic renders against key-on-primed renders and records improved/unchanged/worsened tracks
indexes real diagnostic ares snapshots captured at the last nonzero DSP KON write rather than patching SPC headers after the fact
compares baseline diagnostic renders against real last-key-on renders; current corpus improves 20 tracks, leaves 0 unchanged, and worsens 0

pins the CPU-side C0:ABBD $2140 write, APU-side $062A/$F4 read and zero acknowledgement write, and ares CPU/SMP mailbox bridge entry points as the next faithful replacement target

loads generated APU RAM seeds into ares' real SMP/DSP objects, delivers the track command through a timed smp.portWrite at the $062A command-read boundary, reaches key-on across the representative corpus, and writes real-SMP key-on SPC snapshots

delivers the track command through ares' CPU-side writeAPU($2140, track) bridge at the $062A command-read boundary and reaches command read, zero ack, key-on, and valid SPC snapshots across the representative corpus

renders CPU-side writeAPU snapshots through libgme; the current corpus is 20 audible tracks, unchanged versus direct ares smp.portWrite, and improved on all 20 tracks versus baseline final-state diagnostic snapshots

implemented_outputs.validation: runs and prints ares SFC DSP link smoke OK

known_state.source: cold_start_load_order plus track load_order from audio-pack contract
known_state.details: 64 KiB image after applying LOAD_SPC700_DATA payload blocks in runtime order; semantic transfer metrics replay the cold-start streams and match the generated corpus for 20/20 representative tracks, and the load-apply smoke now starts from bootstrap RAM plus runtime-applied track streams.
Terminal block sends destination $0500 with no payload.
Row-0 sequence/common pack is applied before the requested track packs.

One-based track id is now produced by ROM-derived CHANGE_MUSIC after the real pack-decision branches run with table-derived LOAD_SPC700_DATA call arguments, routed through ROM-derived C0:ABBD to APUIO0, and consumed by the APU driver at the $062A command-read boundary.

missing_state.needed_for: faithful .spc snapshot and deterministic renderer start
correct voices, echo, envelopes, FIR/filter, master volume, and key-on state
driver timing, tempo, note scheduling, and hardware timer counters
faithful transition from loader handshake to active track command
missing_state.known: APUIO0 receives the track id through C0ABBD after ChangeMusic finishes loading packs.
missing_state.missing: final APUIO latch values after the SPC driver acknowledges and starts the song.

missing_state.needed_for: matching emulator-captured SPC snapshots at an exact audio frame boundary

renderer_gates.definition_of_done: Can build and validate 64 KiB RAM seeds for representative tracks from a user ROM.

An external backend job writes a complete SPC snapshot or equivalent captured state combining APU RAM, SPC700 registers, DSP registers, control/timer state, and port state from a real handshake/timing boundary.
ares or snes_spc backend jobs render deterministic PCM/WAV from faithful runtime SPC state and report output hashes using the backend result contract.

backend_strategy.next_step: The native harness now imports verified APU RAM seeds into ares DSP memory, probes the $0500 entry code, advances past the $FD timer wait under a rough timer0 shim, reaches track-specific update code after diagnostic APUIO0 command delivery, emits a structurally valid diagnostic SPC snapshot, confirms all representative tracks issue DSP key-on writes during the probe, captures real last-key-on SPC snapshots at the final nonzero DSP KON write, and records instruction/sequence evidence proving the diagnostic command is read before the last-key-on boundary. Those last-key-on snapshots render all 20 representative tracks as audible through libgme, a stricter on-first-read command injection mode renders 20/20 audible with 0 regressions against that corpus, and the mailbox frontier validates the command-read/zero-ack/key-on ordering for all 20 tracks. A separate ares SMP mailbox smoke now delivers the command through the real smp.portWrite path at the $062A boundary, reaches key-on for all 20 representative tracks, writes valid SPC snapshots, and renders 20/20 audible through libgme with 0 classification regressions against the custom last-key-on corpus. The same corpus also succeeds through ares' CPU-side writeAPU($2140, track) bridge. The previous bridge starts from bootstrap-only APU RAM, executes full ROM-derived CHANGE_MUSIC with real pack-decision branches, applies 58 selected LOAD_SPC700_DATA streams / 188 payload blocks / 531593 bytes from ROM into ares APU RAM with 0 apply errors, computes the one-based command, calls ROM-derived C0:ABBD, routes its STA long $002140 bus write through ares' CPU/APU bridge, matches the load-stub probe at the classification level, and improves all 20 tracks versus baseline. The C0:AB06 loader contract byte-checks the real loader fixture and proves the selected stream totals match load-apply evidence; the byte-handshake corpus executes the same ROM-derived loader through ares' WDC65816 core against a modeled APUIO receiver and validates 58/58 selected stream occurrences across 33 unique packs, including full 64 KiB APU RAM byte equality against semantic reconstruction. The real-IPL frontier executes the same CPU loader against the real ares SMP IPL receiver, validates payload-region equality for 33/33 selected unique packs, and proves pack 1 completes the terminal handoff back to the CPU loader. The post-bootstrap frontier boots pack 1 through the real IPL, keeps the game driver alive, and validates payload-region equality for 32/32 later selected unique pack reloads through the running driver. The continuous track-load frontier boots pack 1 once, loads each representative track's selected pack sequence through the running driver, and validates stable payload-region equality for 20/20 tracks while treating live driver/overlay code as mutable runtime state. The CHANGE_MUSIC-to-continuous-load sequence contract proves that this continuous sequence is exactly the pack order requested by real CHANGE_MUSIC load-stub calls for 20/20 tracks. The newest fusion frontier combines those separate proofs: after real IPL bootstrap, full ROM-derived CHANGE_MUSIC now invokes real C0:AB06 against the live driver, sends the final C0:ABBD command, reaches command-read/ack/key-on, validates 20/20 representative runs with stable payload-region equality, emits 20/20 valid key-on SPC snapshots, and renders 20/20 audible WAVs through libgme. The all-track expansion now validates 191/191 load paths and 191/191 stable payload-region matches, with 190 key-on snapshots and track 4 (NONE2) documented as load-ok/no-key-on; the all-track renderer validates 190/190 backend results and classifies 190/190 renders as audible. The playback/export manifest now records 190 valid rendered tracks, hashes and metrics for app consumption, ROM-derived distribution policy, and the single skipped NONE2 table entry. The dependency policy records ares/libgme/reference-emulator license posture and release gates before public distribution. Gate 2 also moved: the command-write path now uses 0 immediate SMP burst instructions, then observes the driver one SMP instruction at a time until command-read, zero-ack, and key-on. The no-command negative control validates 20/20 captures, stops every track at $055A, and reaches 0 key-on events. Gate 1 is satisfied for the representative corpus, Gate 3 broad-corpus playback/export is now satisfied for snapshot-backed tracks, and the next useful work is continuing Gate 2 by replacing the remaining bounded post-command observation loop with a fuller scheduled runtime.

Out-of-tree libgme now renders job-specified WAVs from all-track fused CHANGE_MUSIC/C0:AB06 key-on SPC snapshots: 190 / 190 snapshot-backed table entries are audible, with NONE2 explicitly skipped as load-ok/no-key-on. Earlier representative diagnostic baselines remain useful history, but the active release path is now fused snapshot playback/export plus emulator-oracle comparison, loop/fade policy, and dependency notice polish.
Compare emitted PCM or captured SPC state against bsnes/higan, Mesen2, or Mednafen without linking them into core tools.

next_manual_seam: Gate 1 is satisfied for the representative corpus, and the all-track fusion path now validates 191/191 track entries, emits 190 key-on snapshots plus one explicit NONE2 no-key-on record, renders 190/190 snapshot-backed tracks, and classifies 190/190 renders as audible. Gate 2 moved from an immediate 128-instruction post-write burst to zero-burst one-instruction observation; keep Gate 2 focused on replacing the remaining bounded post-command loop with fuller CPU/APU scheduling.
